reasoning: The Lean formalization matches the informal statement on all key points. The hypotheses correctly encode: differentiability of f (extended to all of ℝ as a standard formalization convenience since conclusions only depend on x ≥ 0 values), the ODE f'(x) = -3f(x) + 6f(2x) for x > 0, and the decay bound |f(x)| ≤ e^(-√x) for x ≥ 0. The moments μ_n are correctly defined as ∫_{(0,∞)} x^n f(x) dx. Part (a) is encoded via an explicit solution formula μ_0 · n!/(3^n · ∏_{m=1}^n(1-2^{-m})), which is the correct closed form derivable via integration by parts. Part (b) correctly encodes both claims: existence of a limit (convergence) via ∃ L, Tendsto..., and "limit is 0 only if μ_0 = 0" as an implication from Tendsto-to-0 to μ_0 = 0 (matching standard English "A only if B" = A → B). The extension of f from [0,∞) to all of ℝ is a routine formalization technique that doesn't alter the mathematical content.
